methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $58M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

data.narrative:
Verdant Robotics is a Series A robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $58M to date. The valuation assessment draws on a comparable universe of 9 private robotics firms at Series B stage and 1 public comparable, reflecting the concentration of growth-stage capital in this sector.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method, anchoring to total capital raised as a proxy for pre-money value, applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage progression, implying a valuation of $116M. The peer-median method benchmarks against six same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private peers—including 1X Technologies ($10.0B), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B), Dexterity ($1.6B), Collaborative Robotics ($500M), and Fairmat ($350M)—yielding a median valuation of $1.1B and a range from $300M to $10.0B. The wide dispersion reflects heterogeneity in robotics subsegments and investor appetite for outlier opportunities.

Triangulation across both methods supports a valuation range of $99M (low) to $1.2B (high), with a median of $596M. The stage-progression method produces the floor; the peer median substantially exceeds it, driven by elevated Series B valuations in the cohort. Key uncertainties include subsector positioning relative to peers, capital efficiency relative to comparable burn profiles, and whether Verdant's technology and market timing justify positioning closer to the peer median or the stage-derived floor.
